classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “So when they had dined, Jesus saith to Simon Peter, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou Me more than these?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith unto Him, Yea, Lord, Thou knowest that I love Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There are indeed many other things which are able to give us boldness towards God, and to show us bright and approved, but that which most of all brings good will from on high, is tender care for our neighbor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which therefore Christ requireth of Peter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when their eating was ended, Jesus saith to Simon Peter, “Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou Me more than these?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith unto Him, Yea, Lord, Thou knowest that I love Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He saith unto him, Feed My sheep.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why, having passed by the others, doth He speak with Peter on these matters?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He was the chosen one of the Apostles, the mouth of the disciples, the leader of the band; on this account also Paul went up upon a time to enquire of him rather than the others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And at the same time to show him that he must now be of good cheer, since the denial was done away, Jesus putteth into his hands the chief authority among the brethren; and He bringeth not forward the denial, nor reproacheth him with what had taken place, but saith, “If thou lovest Me, preside over thy brethren, and the warm love which thou didst ever manifest, and in which thou didst rejoice, show thou now; and the life which thou saidst thou wouldest lay down for Me, now give for My sheep.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When then having been asked once and again, he called Him to witness who knoweth the secrets of the heart, and then was asked even a third time, he was troubled, fearing a repetition of what had happened before, (for then, having been strong in assertion, he was afterwards convicted,) and therefore he again betaketh himself to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou knowest all things,” meaneth, “things present, and things to come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how he had become better and more sober, being no more self-willed, or contradicting?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For on this account he was troubled, “lest perchance I think that I love, and love not, as before when I thought and affirmed much, yet I was convicted at last.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But Jesus asketh him the third time, and the third time giveth him the same injunction, to show at what a price He setteth the care of His own sheep, and that this especially is a sign of love towards Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And having spoken to him concerning the love towards Himself, He foretelleth to him the martyrdom which he should undergo, showing that He said not to Him what he said as distrusting, but as greatly trusting him; wishing besides to point out a proof of love towards Him, and to instruct us in what manner especially we ought to love Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When thou wast young, thou girdedst thyself, and walkedst whither thou wouldest; but when thou art old, others shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou willest not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet this he did will, and desired; on which account also He hath revealed it to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since Peter had continually said, “I will lay down my life for Thee” ( c. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 37 ), and, “Though I should die with Thee, yet will I not deny Thee” ( Matt. xxvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 35 ): He hath given him back his desire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is the, “Whither thou willest not”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He speaketh of natural feeling, and the necessity of the flesh, and that the soul is unwillingly torn away from the body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that even though the will were firm, yet still even then nature would be found in fault.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For no one lays aside the body without feeling, God, as I said before, having suitably ordained this, that violent deaths might not be many.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if, as things are, the devil has been able to effect this, and has led ten thousand to precipices and pits; had not the soul felt such a desire for the body, the many would have rushed to this under any common discouragement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The, “whither thou willest not,” is then the expression of one signifying natural feeling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how after having said, “When thou wast young,” doth He again say, “When thou art old”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is the expression of one declaring that he was not then young; (nor was he; nor yet old, but a man of middle age. ) Wherefore then did He recall to his memory his former life?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Signifying, that this is the nature of what belongeth to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In things of this life the young man is useful, the old useless; “but in Mine,” He saith, “not so; but when old age hath come on, then is excellence brighter, then is manliness more illustrious, being nothing hindered by the time of life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He said not to terrify, but to rouse Him; for He knew his love, and that he long had yearned for this blessing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At the same time He declareth the kind of death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since Peter ever desired to be in the dangers which were for His sake, “Be of good cheer,” He saith, “I will so satisfy thy desire, that, what thou sufferedst not when young, thou must suffer when thou art old.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then the Evangelist, to rouse the hearer, has added,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “This spake He, signifying by what death he should glorify God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “Should die,” but, “Should glorify God,” that thou mayest learn, that to suffer for Christ, is glory and honor to the sufferer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And when He had spoken this, He saith, Follow Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here again He alludeth to his tender carefulness, and to his being very closely attached to Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if any should say, “How then did James receive the chair at Jerusalem?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I would make this reply, that He appointed Peter teacher, not of the chair, but of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then Peter turning about, seeth the disciple whom Jesus loved following; who also leaned on His breast at supper; and saith, Lord, and what shall this man do?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore hath he reminded us of that reclining?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not without cause or in a chance way, but to show us what boldness Peter had after the denial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he who then did not dare to question Jesus, but committed the office to another, was even entrusted with the chief authority over the brethren, and not only doth not commit to another what relates to himself, but himself now puts a question to his Master concerning another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: John is silent, but Peter speaks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He showeth also here the love which he bare towards him; for Peter greatly loved John, as is clear from what followed, and their close union is shown through the whole Gospel, and in the Acts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore Christ had foretold great things to him, and committed the world to him, and spake beforehand of his martyrdom, and testified that his love was greater than that of the others, desiring to have John also to share with him, he said, “And what shall this man do?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Shall he not come the same way with us?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And as at that other time not being able himself to ask, he puts John forward, so now desiring to make him a return, and supposing that he would desire to ask about the matters pertaining to himself, but had not courage, he himself undertook the questioning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since he spake from strong affection, and wishing not to be torn away from him, Christ, to show that however much he might love, he could not go beyond His love, saith, “If I will that he tarry—what is that to thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By these words teaching us not to be impatient, nor curious beyond what seemeth good to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For because Peter was ever hot, and springing forward to enquiries such as this, to cut short his warmth, and to teach him not to enquire farther, He saith this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then went this saying abroad among the brethren, that that disciple should not die; yet Jesus said not that he shall not die; but, If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Do not thou on any account suppose,” He saith, “that I order your matters after a single rule.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this He did to withdraw them from their unseasonable sympathy for each other; for since they were about to receive the charge of the world, it was necessary that they should no longer be closely associated together; for assuredly this would have been a great loss to the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He saith unto him, “Thou hast had a work entrusted to thee, look to it, accomplish it, labor and struggle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What if I will that he tarry here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Look thou to and care for thine own matters.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe, I pray thee, here also the absence of pride in the Evangelist; for having mentioned the opinion of the disciples, he corrects it, as though they had not comprehended what Jesus meant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus said not,” he tells us, “that ‘he shall not die, but, If I will that he tarry.’”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “This is the disciple which testifieth of these things, and wrote these things, and we know that his testimony is true.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why is it, that then, when none of the others do so, he alone uses these words, and that for the second time, witnessing to himself? for it seems to be offensive to the hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is the cause?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He is said to have been the last who came to writing, Christ having moved and roused him to the work; and on this account he continually sets forth his love, alluding to the cause by which he was impelled to write.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore also he continually makes mention of it, to make his record trustworthy, and to show, that, moved from thence, he came to this work.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I know,” he saith, “that the things are true which he saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if the many believe not, it is permitted them to believe from this.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “From what?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From that which is said next.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “There are also many other things which Jesus did, the which, if they should be written every one, I suppose that even the world itself could not contain the books that should be written.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whence it is clear that I could not have written to court favor; for I who, when the miracles were so many, have not even related so many as the others have, but omitting most of them, have brought forward the plots of the Jews, the stonings, the hatred, the insults, the revilings, and have shown how they called Him a demoniac and a deceiver, certainly could not have acted to gain favor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it behooved one who courted favor to do the contrary, to reject the reproachful, to set forth the glorious.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then he wrote what he did from full assurance, he does not decline to produce his own testimony, challenging men separately to enquire into and scrutinize the circumstances.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is a custom with us, when we think that we are speaking exactly true, never to refuse our testimony; and if we do this, much more would he who wrote by the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then the other Apostles when they preached declared, he also saith; “We are witnesses of the things spoken, and the Spirit which He hath given to them that obey Him.” ( Acts v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And besides, he was present at all, and did not desert Him even when being crucified, and had His mother entrusted to him; all which things are signs of his love for Him, and of his knowing all things exactly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if he has said that so many miracles had taken place, marvel thou not, but, considering the ineffable power of the Doer, receive with faith what is spoken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it was as easy for Him to do whatever He would, as it is for us to speak, or rather much easier; for it sufficed that He should will only, and all followed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then give exact heed to the words, and let us not cease to unfold and search them through, for it is from continual application that we get some advantage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So shall we be able to cleanse our life, so to cut up the thorns; for such a thing is sin and worldly care, fruitless and painful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And as the thorn whatever way it is held pricks the holder, so the things of this life, on whatever side they be laid hold of, give pain to him who hugs and cherishes them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not such are spiritual things; they resemble a pearl, whichever way thou turn it, it delights the eyes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A man hath done a deed of mercy; he not only is fed with hopes of the future, but also is cheered by the good things here, being everywhere full of confidence, and doing all with much boldness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He hath got the better of an evil desire; even before obtaining the Kingdom, he hath already received the fruit here, being praised and approved, before all others, by his own conscience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And every good work is of this nature; just as conscience also punishes wicked deeds here, even before the pit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if, after sinning, thou considerest the future, thou becomest afraid and tremblest, though no man punish thee; if the present, thou hast many enemies, and livest in suspicion, and canst not henceforth even look in the face those who have wronged thee, or rather, those who have not wronged thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For we do not in the case of those evil deeds reap so much pleasure, as we do despondency, when conscience cries out against us, men, without, condemn us, God is angered, the pit travailing to receive us, our thoughts not at rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A heavy, a heavy and a burdensome thing is sin, harder to bear than any lead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He at least who hath any sense of it will not be able to look up ever so little, though he be very dull.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus, for instance, Ahab, though very impious, when he felt this, walked bending downwards, crushed and afflicted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account he clothed himself in sackcloth, and shed fountains of tears. ( 1 Kings xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we do this, and grieve as he did, we shall put off our faults as did Zacchæus, and we too shall obtain some pardon. ( Luke xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as in the case of tumors, and fistulous ulcers, if one stay not first the discharge which runs over and inflames the wound, how many soever remedies he applies, while the source of the evil is not stopped, he doth all in vain; so too if we stay not our hand from covetousness, and check not that evil afflux of wealth, although we give alms, we do all to no purpose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that which was healed by it, covetousness coming after is wont to overwhelm and spoil, and to make harder to heal than before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then cease from rapine, and so do alms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if we betake ourselves to precipices, how shall we be able to recover ourselves? for if one party (that is, alms-doing) were to pull at a falling man from above, while another was forcibly dragging him from below, the only result of such a struggle would be, that the man would be torn asunder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That we may not suffer this, nor, while covetousness weighs us down from below, alms-doing depart and leave us, let us lighten ourselves, and spread our wings, that having been perfected by the riddance of evil things, and the practice of good, we may obtain the goods everlasting, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, dominion, and honor, now and ever and world without end.
